title: Founding a Hardware Simulation SaaS Platform @ Sahara Cloud

blocks.text: Co-founded Sahara Cloud, a web-based hardware simulation and remote testing platform that lets engineering teams build, test, and debug hardware in the cloud. Owned product, customer discovery, MVP definition, and GTM — raising $750K pre-seed and securing pilots across universities and enterprise customers.
Problem
Software, firmware, and hardware testing lived in disconnected tools.
Teams used makeshift scripts to glue simulation, debugging, and testing together.
Limited physical hardware access created bottlenecks and high CapEx.
Debugging cycles stretched R&D timelines and cost.
My Role
Co-founder and Chief Product Officer — owned product, discovery, and roadmap.
Led MVP definition, pilot design, and early customer development.
Supported fundraising with market validation and product narrative.
Product Approach
Validated workflow pain through 50+ discovery interviews before scoping the MVP.
Narrowed the wedge to remote access, simulation, and debugging.
Designed for university and enterprise pilots in parallel for fast learning.
Backed positioning with A/B testing on funnel conversion.
Solution

items.title: Remote hardware access
items.description: Shared cloud access to physical hardware for distributed teams.

items.title: Cloud simulation
items.description: Run hardware simulation workflows without local infrastructure.

items.title: Shared environments
items.description: Team-level testing environments with consistent state.

items.title: Pilot onboarding flow
items.description: Lightweight onboarding tuned to academic and enterprise pilot needs.

blocks.text: Key Product Decisions

blocks.tradeoff: Narrowed to remote testing and simulation; passed on replacing every design tool.
blocks.outcome: Clear wedge with measurable pilot traction.

blocks.tradeoff: Used universities for fast validation; pursued enterprise for commercial proof.
blocks.outcome: Two channels of evidence supporting the fundraise.

blocks.tradeoff: Built only the workflows needed to validate willingness to engage.
blocks.outcome: Pilots launched fast and informed roadmap priorities.

blocks.tradeoff: Supported pilot-specific needs while extracting repeatable patterns.
blocks.outcome: Reusable platform foundation emerged from real customer use.

blocks.text: Results & Impact
Raised $750K in pre-seed funding.
Launched MVP with 200 beta users across 3 universities and 1 enterprise.
Secured 4 pilot engagements across academic and enterprise customers.
Achieved 30% prospect-to-demo rate through sharper positioning and outreach.
Increased sign-up conversion by 50% through A/B testing and onboarding improvements.
Modeled a potential 35% reduction in R&D time and up to $1M in customer cost savings based on pilot workflows.
Metrics Tracked
Beta users
Pilot engagements
Prospect-to-demo conversion
Sign-up conversion
Discovery interviews
Fundraising milestones
Key Learnings
Founder-led product needs a painful wedge customers will validate fast.
Early pilots must balance custom support with repeatable platform patterns.
Fundraising compounds when product vision is backed by pilot evidence.
